remember: Students will be able to identify and explain the concept of infrastructure and its importance in the economy.
Students will be able to recall and describe the different types of infrastructure, such as transport, communication, and energy infrastructure.
Students will be able to define and explain the role of public and private sectors in providing infrastructure.
understand: Students will be able to analyze the impact of infrastructure on economic growth and development.
Students will be able to explain how infrastructure affects the standard of living and quality of life of people.
Students will be able to describe the ways in which infrastructure can be planned and managed effectively.
apply: Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of different infrastructure projects and policies.
Students will be able to apply economic concepts to real-world infrastructure problems and issues.
Students will be able to suggest improvements to existing infrastructure and propose new infrastructure projects.
analyze: Students will be able to critically examine the relationship between infrastructure and economic development.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of infrastructure on different sectors of the economy, such as agriculture and industry.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of infrastructure in achieving sustainable development and reducing inequality.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of infrastructure and its importance in the economy.
Describe the different types of infrastructure and their role in economic development.
Discuss the role of public and private sectors in providing infrastructure.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the concept of infrastructure and its importance in the economy (CBSE Class 11 Economics Chapter 6).
Describe the different types of infrastructure and their role in economic development (CBSE Class 11 Economics Chapter 6).
Discuss the impact of infrastructure on economic growth and development (CBSE Class 11 Economics Chapter 6).
